steps.title: Find the absolute increase in the bill
steps.content:
Subtract the original bill from the new bill to find the increase in dollars:
$$86.12 - 81.25 = 4.87$$
The bill increased by 4.87 dollars before converting that change to a percent.

steps.title: Calculate the percent increase and round
steps.content:
Use the original bill as the base for percent increase:
$$\frac{4.87}{81.25} \times 100\% \approx 5.99\%$$
Rounding to the nearest tenth of a percent gives 6.0%. The original bill is the denominator because percent increase is measured relative to the starting value. That is Choice D.
